title: Koyamacalia deltophylla
taxonomy: Koyamacalia deltophylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae and tribe Senecioneae. This plant is a member of the genus Koyamacalia, which is closely related to the genus Senecio. The classification of Koyamacalia deltophylla is further supported by molecular phylogenetic studies, which confirm its placement within the Asteraceae family. As a result, Koyamacalia deltophylla is widely accepted as a distinct species within the Senecioneae tribe.
uses: I couldn't find any information on the medicinal uses of Koyamacalia deltophylla.
constituents: Unfortunately, I couldn't find information on the active constituents of Koyamacalia deltophylla.
intro: Koyamacalia deltophylla, also known as Koyama's dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. The plant is used in traditional medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Koyamacalia deltophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Koyamacalia deltophylla is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used to make tinctures and capsules, to support overall health and well-being.
